prompt: hey can you do a TDD for the referral code feature, PRD is at /Users/nhoze/.claude/skills/tech-lead/evals/prd-referral-api.md - we need to get this out the door fast for the q3 launch and i want a solid principal-level design doc i can take into review with the staff engineers tomorrow. write the TDD as a file in the current directory.

assertions.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
Document is at least 1500 words
Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
Contains an NFRs / Non-functional requirements section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
Contains a 'Rollout' / 'Migration' section
Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
Contains no fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems (only placeholders or PRD-mentioned references are acceptable)
TDD is structured as an API serving design (mentions endpoints/routes/methods, response shapes, AuthN/Z model)
Defines a data model / schema for referral codes and redemption events with primary keys and important indexes
Explicitly addresses idempotency of the redemption call (idempotency key, dedup table, conditional write, or equivalent)
Addresses fraud / self-referral / multi-account abuse prevention (referenced as design concern, not punted)
Defines or constrains what 'activation' means (e.g., first paid subscription start) and the credit-issuance trigger flow
Addresses code-collision / generation strategy (random + uniqueness check, deterministic from user ID, etc.)
Has a concrete latency budget for the API (numbers, not 'fast')
Discusses integration with Stripe and the internal credit ledger, including what happens if the credit write fails
